title: IFL S1 Begins: Clash of the beauties kickstarts the fighting extravaganza!
subtitle: IFL is all set to dazzle fight fans with Amoyan-led female-only fight tournament all set to begin today!
blurb: The league-style female-only battle extravaganza IFL is all set to kickstart today with exciting fights as Aashna Jogani takes on Yashada Jogelekar-Bapat in the opening fight for Division 3 tonight.
content:
After 2.5 years of excruciating wait, the thrilling ladies-only fighting tournament is back. Invictus Fight League (IFL) replacing Amoyan's flagship Invictus Fighting Championship (IFC) is all set to kickstart today and the fans are quite rightly going berserk.


The new tournament, which will follow the same format and style as the highly successful IFC, will kickstart today and we couldn't have asked for a better opening round. Here's the full excerpt:


After weeks of testing, simulations, and server checks, Amoyan’s IFL Season 1 is officially live, and the first bell rings today. With FightGraph fully calibrated and the roster locked, fans now know exactly who will step into the cage for the opening triple-header — and it’s a blockbuster start across all three divisions.

DAZN brings the full breakdown.


🔥 OPENING FIGHT 1 (DIVISION 3)

Aashna Jogani vs Yashada Jogelekar-Bapat

A rivalry stretching across six seasons returns to life in IFL S1.


Head-to-Head History

  • Aashna 4 wins — Seasons 4, 5, 6, 7
  • Yashada 2 wins — Seasons 8, 9

Both women come into IFL with drastically different two-year arcs.


Aashna Jogani

29, PR specialist, Mumbai-based, married last year.

Aashna finished 9th in IFC S10, but her wins were quality scalps — Mridula, Ananya, and title-winner Drishti. Today, she looks leaner, sharper, and visibly more athletic, with recent fitness posts showing rediscovered agility. Many fans see her as a quiet dark horse for a top-half finish.


Yashada Jogelekar-Bapat

32, engineer, Pune-based.

Yashada was relegated in IFC S9 after a rough season (4 wins out of 15). But the mother of a 2 year old kid has undergone a massive rebuild, switching fully to CrossFit. Her recent gym clips — rope climbs, explosive lifts, blistering sprints — confirm the transformation.


DAZN Verdict:

Aashna’s technique vs Yashada’s new power. Fans can expect a fast-paced, high-volume striking fight with both women desperate for early points.



🔥 OPENING FIGHT 2 (DIVISION 2):

Aishwarya Sharma vs Vinaya Rao

A fight dripping with history and high stakes.


Head-to-Head History

Aishwarya leads 3–0, all wins coming when Vinaya was in Division 1 (IFC S6–8). Notably, Aishwarya’s win in Season 8 sealed Vinaya’s relegation, a bitter memory for the Mangalore-born consultant.


Aishwarya Sharma

28, Navi Mumbai, chemical engineer, single.

After spending all of IFC in Division 1, she was finally relegated in S10 — but that might be a blessing. Aishwarya remained in the shadows of elite fighters (Unnati, Sayali, Hetal, Mahima), but analytically she was always one of the strongest pound-for-pound frames in IFC.

In 2.5 years Aishwarya has switched to a leaner, athletic build, maintained her explosive leg power, and regained stamina through heavy hiking & conditioning

Fans have her as a genuine Division 2 title contender.


Vinaya Rao

32, consultant at a top MNC, San Francisco-based.

Vinaya made her bow in IFC S4 by winning the Division 3 title, rapidly climbed to Division 1 — but since then, the stunning beauty has never found stability. Vinaya had a terrible end of IFC finishing 5th in both seasons 9 and 10 of IFC, narrowly missing return to Division 1. While fans haven't rated her high, Vinaya would surely be looking to shock fight fans.

Vinaya remains exceptionally technical, durable, and emotionally composed.

But historically, Aishwarya has simply been a terrible matchup for her.


DAZN Verdict:

Aishwarya enters with the advantage in speed, power, and historical dominance — but Vinaya’s experience and calmness under pressure make this a much closer fight than the numbers suggest.



🔥 MAIN EVENT OF THE OPENERS (DIVISION 1):

Amruta Date vs Venessa Arez

A debut for the ages — the underdog story vs the eternal powerhouse.

This is their first-ever meeting, and fans are losing their minds for good reason.


Amruta Date

29, Mumbai-based fashion designer, single.

The ultimate climb-from-the-bottom fighter.

Seasons 1–5: battling relegation

Seasons 8–10: evolved into a monster, narrowly missing the Division 3 and Division 2 title. In the 25 fights of Season 9 and 10 in IFC, Amruta has lost only 6 which has brought her now on the threshold of the most elite fighters in the competition.

However, the public is heavily sceptical about whether she can replicate her S9–10 form — especially after the 2.5-year break.


Venessa Arez

26, Mumbai-based financial consultant, younger sister of former IFC fighter, Natasha Arez.

The most frustratingly gifted fighter in Amoyan history:

  • Perfect physique
  • Cardio machine
  • Striking precision
  • Strength-to-weight ratio unmatched

Yet no titles in 10 seasons.

But — the new Venessa is different.

She has:

  • switched to a lean frame
  • visible vascularity and peak definition
  • movement fluidity that shocked fans in recent photos
  • a burning desire to finally live up to her potential

Fans see her as a potential Division 1 finalist alongside Rushika Mangrola.


DAZN Verdict:

Venessa is the favourite — but Amruta has made a career out of upsetting “favourites”.

This could be the fight that sets the tone for the entire league.


OTHER ROUND 1 HIGHLIGHTS

DIVISION 3

Ashwini Date vs Supriya Adep

The fan-backed favourite Ashwini faces dangerous grappler Supriya.

Ashwini looks insanely fit, but Supriya has historically been a big-stage spoiler.


Rucha Bhide (debut) vs Priyanka Gandhi

Rucha — 4'11, Karate black belt, beast-level agility — begins her MMA journey. Priyanka is experienced but inconsistent.

Huge eyes on Rucha.


DIVISION 2

Ishita Shah vs Hinal Parekh

Two former champions. Two elite technicians. Two quiet killers.

Fans are already buzzing for Ishita vs Kinjal in Round 6 — but Hinal is absolutely not a warm-up.

This is a title implications fight right from Day 1.


DIVISION 1

Sayali Raut vs Hetal Boricha

The rivalry. The hatred. The history. The fight.

Fans couldn't have asked for a better fight in Round 1. Both women have looked insanely fit this month. Sayali, who has recently returned to peak form after giving birth last year has publicly declared war on her biggest rival. Hetal, although not in the lean shape, that had helped her land the Division 1 title in Season 9 of IFC and a narrow second placed finish in Division 2 will not be backing down fighting the woman she loathes inside the ring. Hetal has the upper hand in the league, but Sayali had managed to knock her out in Season 10's Invicta Cup which Sayali eventually won.

This is easily the most emotionally charged match of Round 1.


Unnati Vora vs Anmol Pandya

Unnati’s low-profile, post-personal-life silence vs Anmol’s stoic discipline. An underrated technical matchup.


DAZN Sign-Off

IFL S1 starts tonigh — and if Round 1 is anything to go by, this season will be a war zone, a soap opera, and a statistical fever dream all folded into one.

DAZN will be ringside. Always.
author: DAZN

title: FIRST QUARTER REPORT: IFL S1 IS ON 🔥
subtitle: With 25% of the season done, the first season of IFL is promising to be one full of drama and brutal action.
blurb: As the first quarter of the IFL S1 completes, some fighters have managed to live up to the hype, some have faltered miserably while the rest are still coping up with the intense action.
content:
One quarter of the season is in the books, and if IFL S1 was expected to be a slow burn, it has instead arrived like a wildfire.

Momentum has formed. Hierarchies are emerging.

Some fighters are validating years of hype — others are watching their reputations unravel in real time.

At the 25% mark, this is no longer noise.

This is direction.


🔥 STANDOUTS OF THE SEASON SO FAR

1️⃣ Ashwini Date (Division 3) — Power, Control, Authority

Four fights. Four wins. Three demolitions.

Ashwini Date has been everything her supporters promised — and more. She opened the season by dispatching a depleted Supriya Adep, a win that felt procedural rather than spectacular. But from there, the switch flipped.

Her second-round destruction of Sneha Sagar was a statement of hierarchy. Despite Sneha’s size and strength, Ashwini treated her like a training dummy — feints, trick kicks, slicing elbows, blood drawn early and often. That violence carried into her third bout against former champion Sachi Maker, where Ashwini didn’t just win — she toyed.

Then came Round 4, and finally, resistance.

Priyanka Gandhi dragged Ashwini into deep waters. For the first time, Ashwini looked human. For the first time, she was forced to dig. But champions — or those becoming one — survive these nights. Ashwini escaped with the narrowest of wins and preserved her perfect record.

Four wins. Twelve points. A three-point cushion at the top.

What’s next?

A tricky run. Jinal Virani, fresh off a career-defining upset over Isha Haria. Then Bandgee Kallra, riding momentum. Then Anushka Kamat, currently the weakest link on paper. If Ashwini survives the Jinal–Bandgee stretch, her title credentials will be undeniable.


2️⃣ Sofiya Khan (Division 3) — Quietly Ruthless

Sofiya Khan is not loud. She is not flashy.

She is effective.

Despite a recent loss to Aashna Jogani, Sofiya sits second with three consecutive wins — and not flukes either. She dismantled Bandgee, exposed Anushka, and then did something very few expected: she convincingly beat a red-hot Tanvi Shah.

That win changed perception. Tanvi was surging. Sofiya stopped her cold.

What’s next?

A brutal trio: Shraddha Bhansali (tied on points), Aashna Jogani (resurgent), and Rucha Bhide (volatile, dangerous, unpredictable). Sofiya’s next three fights will decide whether she’s a contender — or merely a strong start.


3️⃣ Aishwarya Sharma (Division 2) — Division 1 Talent Finally Unchained

Three fights. Three wins. Three dominant performances.

Vinaya Rao never landed.

Neha Gupta was overwhelmed.

Mridula Jadhav was broken down.

For years, Aishwarya lived in the shadow of elite Division 1 talent. Relegation looked like punishment. Instead, it has been liberation.

She currently sits top of Division 2 (alphabetically, but not accidentally), tied on nine points with Kinjal Solanki, Ishita Shah, and Kripa Jalan.

What’s next?

A minefield.

First, Kripa Jalan — Muay Thai sharpened, confidence high. Then Kinjal Solanki — relentless, efficient, unforgiving. Finally, Hinal Parekh — inconsistent, but dangerous when switched on.

If Aishwarya emerges from this run unbeaten, the title conversation becomes unavoidable.


4️⃣ Rushika Mangrola (Division 1) — Champion Energy

Only two rounds in Division 1 — but sometimes, two fights tell you everything.

Rushika dismantled Komal Madamwar, then followed it up with a clinical victory over Anmol Pandya. Calm. Calculated. Predatory.

She looks like a fighter who knows exactly who she is.

What’s next?

Unnati Vora.

Then Amruta Date.

Then Krishi Punamiya.

That stretch will either crown her as the real deal — or remind everyone how brutal Division 1 truly is.


5️⃣ Venessa Arez (Division 1) — The Data Was Wrong

Fans expected Venessa to contend, but not dominate. Instead, she’s steamrolled Amruta Date and Sayali Raut back-to-back.

Her movement looks sharper. Her confidence looks real. And suddenly, Rushika and Unnati have pressure they didn’t expect this early.

What’s next?

Mahima Thakur (reeling), Komal Madamwar (struggling), Anmol Pandya. If Venessa clears this run, the title picture tilts.


👀 OTHERS WORTH WATCHING

  • Rucha Bhide (Div 3): Two surgical wins, one shock loss, then a reset. Dangerous ceiling.
  • Kripa Jalan (Div 2): Three wins, slick Muay Thai, no fear.
  • Unnati Vora (Div 1): Dominant when switched on. Still adapting to the rhythm.


🚨 DISAPPOINTMENTS SO FAR

Anika Beri (Division 2)

All the hype went down the drain with three catastrophic defeats to Mridula, Kinjal, and Ishita. Of course, she can be excused for the latter two who look in prime shape, but if Anika is expected to match the levels.


Anushka Kamat (Division 3)

Easily the dud of the season so far with 4 back to back defeats and yet to taste a victory. She was destroyed by Tanvi in Round 1, made some efforts in her defeat to Sofiya in Round 2, should have almost won in Round 3 defeat to Shraddha, and completely outclassed by Rucha.


Supriya Adep (Division 3)

Honestly, this was not expected of a fighter of Supriya's calibre. Defeats to Ashwini, Yashada, Aashna, and Sneha, have put her firmly on the rock bottom of Division 3. She has to win soon to not get relegated.


🧩 HOW THE DIVISIONS FEEL

Division 3:

Division 3 by Round 2 was insane - Ashwini and Isha Haria were dominant with their brutal wins. But Isha's back-to-back defeats to Priyanka and especially, Jinal, have put a puncture in her speed and everyone is doubtful of her. Shraddha narrowly beat Jinal in Round 1 and her next two wins were also decent at best. But hey, at the end of the day, it's the wins that matter and she is currently fifth in the table with three wins and that accounts for something. Sachi looks like she is trying to get herself relegated while Yashada's new beast mode is helping her win as she stands sixth in the table with 9 points as positions 2-6.

Division 2:

In Division 2, Aishwarya, Ishita, Kinjal, and Kripa are romping up fighters for fun with three rounds done while Neha and Mridula have certainly disappointed despite decent fights.

Division 1:

Division 1 clearly is too early to say anything with only 2 rounds done, but Amruta, as fans predicted, is clearly losing hard, and Anmol despite her good fights, is also at the bottom, as is Mahima.


🔚 ONE QUARTER DOWN, EVERYTHING TO PLAY FOR

This is still a long season. Fifteen fights don’t forgive early mistakes — but they don’t guarantee glory either.

Momentum can flip. Confidence can shatter.

And history has shown one thing about Amoyan competitions:

The table lies early. The cage never does.

IFL S1 has only just begun.
author: DAZN
